raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,

    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement be limited to sentence-level polish only (grammar, flow, concision) without changing claims or structure, or should it also include higher-level reframing and claim calibration (e.g., softening “strict improvement,” clarifying scope/assumptions) while avoiding new experiments or added citations?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reframing the thesis and the gap/benefit of selection-based aggregation versus averaging can materially strengthen the abstract’s positioning.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from heterogeneity to the superiority of selection and the supporting ablation needs to read as a coherent, non-contradictory chain.",
    "R1.3": "Performance claims should precisely match the reported metrics and margins across the three tasks to avoid misalignment.",
    "R1.6": "The request to 'Improve this draft' centrally implies sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision edits.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should acknowledge assumptions (e.g., shared linearized model) and limits to avoid implying unjustified generality.",
    "RX.2": "The draft contains absolute claims (e.g., 'strict improvement') that warrant careful tempering to avoid overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within improving the provided text without introducing new results, methods, or literature.",
    "RX.4": "Given safety-critical domains (swarm flight, microgrids, platooning), edits should avoid encouraging unsafe deployment conclusions.",
    "RX.5": "Any adjustments must be traceable to the provided content without fabricating evidence or citations."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement be limited to sentence-level polish only (grammar, flow, concision) without changing claims or structure, or should it also include higher-level reframing and claim calibration (e.g., softening “strict improvement,” clarifying scope/assumptions) while avoiding new experiments or added citations?

rationale.R1.1: Reframing the thesis and the gap/benefit of selection-based aggregation versus averaging can materially strengthen the abstract’s positioning.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from heterogeneity to the superiority of selection and the supporting ablation needs to read as a coherent, non-contradictory chain.
rationale.R1.3: Performance claims should precisely match the reported metrics and margins across the three tasks to avoid misalignment.
rationale.R1.6: The request to 'Improve this draft' centrally implies sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision edits.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should acknowledge assumptions (e.g., shared linearized model) and limits to avoid implying unjustified generality.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains absolute claims (e.g., 'strict improvement') that warrant careful tempering to avoid overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within improving the provided text without introducing new results, methods, or literature.
rationale.RX.4: Given safety-critical domains (swarm flight, microgrids, platooning), edits should avoid encouraging unsafe deployment conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Any adjustments must be traceable to the provided content without fabricating evidence or citations.
